title: Databoxer
description: A cross-platform command-line file encryption utility focused on speed, safety, and usability. Files are encrypted into a custom .box file format with per-file nonces, checksums and additional security measures to ensure data integrity and confidentiality. Intended as a lightweight alternative for protecting local files, removable drives, and transferred data.

title: Copper
description: A lightweight build system for C and C++ projects designed as a simpler alternative to CMake and Makefiles. Copper uses a single configuration file and automatically discovers and compiles sources while still allowing detailed control over compilation and linking. Built around modular “units” to keep large projects readable and maintainable.

title: Feather Chat
description: A lightweight multithreaded client-server chat application built on raw TCP sockets. A central server routes real-time messages between connected clients, supporting direct messaging and planned chat rooms and encryption. Designed to explore networking, concurrency, and scalable message handling without heavy frameworks.

title: Pasteshelf
description: A Pastebin-style web backend that allows users to create accounts and store shareable text pastes identified by unique hash IDs. Exposes a REST API for creating, retrieving, and managing pastes with authentication and database persistence. Can run locally or through Docker with a bundled PostgreSQL instance.

title: Signsense
description: An Android mobile application that translates finger-spelling sign language into readable text using real-time camera recognition. The app uses a custom-trained machine learning model to detect dactyl hand gestures and converts recognized symbols into words directly on the device. Includes live detection, multilingual UI, and configurable recognition settings.

title: Win-Tools
description: A collection of native Windows command-line utilities that re-implement popular UNIX tools such as ls, grep, head, and touch. Built directly on the Windows API to provide better performance and a more native experience than typical ports. Focuses on systems programming, performance, and platform-specific tooling.

title: Simple Rust Kernel
description: A hobby operating system kernel written entirely in Rust and runnable inside QEMU. The project explores low-level systems programming, memory handling, and boot processes using the Rust bootloader ecosystem and custom test runner. Demonstrates OS fundamentals including kernel startup, hardware abstraction, and bare-metal execution.

title: Java DSA
description: A study and reference project implementing classical data structures and algorithms with a custom interface hierarchy. Structures such as stacks, lists, and collections are implemented with multiple underlying representations (e.g., array-based and linked versions) to demonstrate design tradeoffs. Designed similarly to the Java Collections framework to emphasize abstraction and reusability.

title: Bybit Bot
description: An automated crypto trading bot that streamlines Bybit P2P transactions by programmatically handling order processing and payment execution. The system connects to the official Bybit API to monitor incoming and outgoing P2P orders, extract transaction details, and automatically complete payments through Payeer. A key feature is a fully reverse-engineered Payeer integration, including manual reconstruction of private API endpoints, session handling, CSRF tokens, and signature generation. Designed for high reliability and asynchronous performance using Tokio.
